questions.question: Explain why objects fall towards the Earth.
questions.answer: Objects fall towards the Earth because of the gravitational force of attraction between the Earth and the objects.

questions.question: State the factors on which gravitational force depends.
questions.answer: Gravitational force depends on the masses of the two objects and the distance between them.

questions.question: What happens to gravitational force when the distance between two objects is doubled?
questions.answer: When the distance is doubled, the gravitational force becomes one-fourth of its original value.

questions.question: Define acceleration due to gravity and write its value on Earth.
questions.answer: Acceleration due to gravity is the acceleration gained by an object due to Earth's gravity. Its value on Earth is about 9.8 m/s².

questions.question: Why do all objects fall with the same acceleration in vacuum?
questions.answer: All objects fall with the same acceleration in vacuum because acceleration due to gravity is independent of the mass of the object.

questions.question: Distinguish between mass and weight.
questions.answer: Mass is the amount of matter in an object and remains constant everywhere. Weight is the gravitational force acting on the object and changes with location.

questions.question: Why is the weight of an object on the Moon less than on Earth?
questions.answer: The gravitational pull of the Moon is weaker than that of Earth, so the weight becomes one-sixth of its value on Earth.

questions.question: Define thrust and pressure.
questions.answer: Thrust is the force acting perpendicular to a surface. Pressure is defined as thrust per unit area.

questions.question: Why are sharp knives used for cutting?
questions.answer: Sharp knives have a smaller area, which produces greater pressure for the same force and makes cutting easier.

questions.question: Explain buoyancy.
questions.answer: Buoyancy is the upward force exerted by a fluid on an object immersed in it.

questions.question: Why do objects float in water?
questions.answer: Objects float when the buoyant force acting on them is equal to or greater than their weight.

questions.question: Why do camels have broad feet?
questions.answer: Camels have broad feet to increase surface area and reduce pressure on sand, helping them walk easily.

questions.question: State Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: When an object is immersed in a fluid, it experiences an upward buoyant force equal to the weight of the fluid displaced.

questions.question: Explain why ships float on water.
questions.answer: Ships float because they displace a large volume of water which produces enough buoyant force to balance their weight.

questions.question: What is apparent weight?
questions.answer: Apparent weight is the reduced weight of an object when immersed in a fluid due to buoyant force.

questions.question: Name two instruments based on Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: Hydrometer and lactometer are based on Archimedes' principle.

questions.question: Why does an iron nail sink in water?
questions.answer: An iron nail sinks because its density is greater than that of water.

questions.question: Why does cork float on water?
questions.answer: Cork floats because its density is less than that of water.

questions.question: Why does a balloon rise in air?
questions.answer: A balloon rises because the buoyant force of air acting on it is greater than its weight.

questions.question: Explain the relation between thrust and pressure.
questions.answer: Pressure is directly proportional to thrust and inversely proportional to the area on which the thrust acts.

questions.question: Why are foundations of buildings made wide?
questions.answer: Wide foundations reduce pressure on the ground by increasing the area of contact.

questions.question: Why do submarines adjust ballast tanks?
questions.answer: Submarines adjust ballast tanks to change their density and control buoyancy.

questions.question: Why does g decrease with altitude?
questions.answer: The value of g decreases with altitude because the distance from the center of the Earth increases.

questions.question: Why do astronauts appear weightless in space?
questions.answer: Astronauts appear weightless because they are in continuous free fall around the Earth.

questions.question: Explain why large ships float but small nails sink.
questions.answer: Ships float because they displace enough water to produce sufficient buoyant force, whereas nails displace very little water.
